This stage is the system’s front door: the startup layer where argv is parsed, top-level policy is enforced, and execution is routed into the right runtime. `cli/src/main.rs` is the central `codex` entrypoint, selecting between interactive TUI use, non-interactive exec flows, app-server and auth tooling, MCP/plugin management, sandbox utilities, desktop launch, remote control, and diagnostics. Shared command types live in `cli/src/lib.rs`, while `exec/src/cli.rs`, `tui/src/cli.rs`, and `cloud-tasks/src/cli.rs` define the typed command surfaces for their respective binaries.

Several files then carry out specialized launch paths. `tui/src/main.rs` starts the standalone TUI binary, merges config overrides, and reports final status. `cloud-tasks/src/lib.rs` runs the cloud-tasks tool, from backend setup through subcommands and its interactive loop. Desktop opening is split across `cli/src/app_cmd.rs` and `cli/src/desktop_app/*`, which hide macOS and Windows install/open differences behind one launcher. `cli/src/remote_control_cmd.rs`, `cli/src/debug_sandbox.rs`, and `cli/src/sandbox_setup.rs` expose operational and debugging surfaces. `cli/src/doctor.rs` plus `doctor/thread_inventory.rs` implement health checks, while `tui/src/session_archive_commands.rs`, `chatgpt/src/apply_command.rs`, and `cli/src/mcp_cmd.rs` provide focused top-level commands built on shared config and backend services.
